t_ch: 你看上去和听上去都不太危险。
t_en: You don't look or sound that dangerous.

t_ch: 真的吗？宝了个贝的，如果你现在觉得不够刺激，我倒是不介意展示一下什么叫真正的危险和癫狂。
t_en: Really? Fork you. If you think this isn't intense enough, I don't mind showing you what real danger and madness look like.

t_ch: 不瞒你说，我之所以成为法外狂徒并不因为我他宝贝的是一颗炸弹，而是因为我他宝贝的掌握着一则古老而疯狂、为人伦所不容的亵渎咒语。
t_en: To be frank, I didn't become an outlaw because I'm a fudgin' bomb. It's because I'm the fudgin' owner of an ancient, mad, forbidden curse.

t_ch: 你想感受一下吗？我可以念给你听，但一切后果花火大人概不负责。
t_en: Care to experience it? I can chant it for you, but Miss Sparkle won't be responsible for any of the consequences.

t_ch: 让我试试你的斤两！
t_en: Let's see what you've got!

t_ch: 嗬，宝贝，你比我狂多了。让我酝酿酝酿……
t_en: Holy fudge, you're more insane than I am! Let me gather my wits...

t_ch: 算了吧，我惜命。
t_en: Forget it. I value my life too much.

t_ch: 嗬，宝贝，怂了？告诉你，晚了！你已经打开了魔盒……
t_en: Oh? You scared, lil' fudgehead? It's too late, I tell you! You've already opened Pandora's box...

t_ch: …仪式准备好了。竖起耳朵吧，仔细聆听那最古老、最原始、他宝贝的人类刻入基因的战栗——
t_en: ...The ritual is ready. Perk up your ears and listen closely to the most ancient, primal fear etched into the very fudgin' genes of humanity—

t_ch: <size=28>「花斯卡，火斯卡，小花火——！花斯卡，火斯卡，小～花～火～！」</size>
t_en: <size=28>Bloomska, boomska, little sparkleeesss!!! Bloomska, boomska, little sparkleeesss!!!</size>

t_ch: 这酷似匹诺康尼传奇动画品牌主人公「钟表小子」的声线登时令你警铃大作！远古深刻的肌肉记忆令你抡圆了胳膊，将娃娃抛向九霄云外——
t_en: The striking resemblance to the voice of the legendary Penaconian cartoon character "Clockie" instantly rings an alarm within you! The profound muscle memory from ancient times prompts you to swing your arms in a wide arc, catapulting the doll into the heavens.

t_ch: ——如果那娃娃方才所言不虚，那应该确实不会有一枚虚数中子弹在「黄金的时刻」地表爆炸。放心…吧？
t_en: If the doll's words just now are not false, then indeed there won't be an imaginary neutron bomb exploding on the surface at Golden Hour. No need to worry...?

t_ch: 你好，人类。我们又见面了，真没想到会在这里再见到你。
t_en: Hello, human. We meet again. I never thought I'd see you here.

t_ch: 我正在这艘豪华游轮上品尝匹诺康尼消费主义糖衣炮弹。你呢？是来观光的吗？
t_en: I'm currently on this luxury cruise ship enjoying Penacony's consumer-friendly sugar-coated cannonballs. What about you? Are you just seeing the sights?

t_ch: 我是来拆炸弹的。
t_en: I'm here to defuse a bomb.

t_ch: 炸弹？天啊，我可不是炸弹。
t_en: Bomb? Gosh, I'm not a bomb.

t_ch: 我们在哪里见过吗？
t_en: Have we met somewhere?

t_ch: 如果你也去过「仙舟」罗浮，那我们很可能见过面。
t_en: If you've been to the Xianzhou Luofu, it's likely we've met.

t_ch: 你让我想起一位故人。
t_en: You remind me of an old acquaintance.

t_ch: 故人？不，我不是人。
t_en: Old acquaintance? I'm not even human.

t_ch: 我是一只「岁阳」，原本住在罗浮的绥园里。我热衷于研究仙舟亚文化，但慢慢地，我发现仙舟亚文化也是有极限的。
t_en: I'm a heliobus, originally from the Luofu's Fyxestroll Garden. I'm passionate about researching Xianzhou subcultures, but I've slowly come to realize that Xianzhou subcultures also have their limits.

t_ch: 前略，中间忘了，总之我最终来到了匹诺康尼。我想要研究与仙舟完全不同的亚文化，结果可真是让我大跌眼镜——如果我有眼睛的话。
t_en: I forgot what happened, but anyway, I ended up in Penacony. I wanted to study a subculture that is completely different from the Xianzhou's, and boy it's really been an eye-opening experience — that is, if I had eyes.

t_ch: 我发现，这里的人们不把亚文化当文化。他们管它叫迷…迷……
t_en: I've come to realize, the people here don't treat subcultures as actual culture. They call them me... me...

t_ch: 迷因？
t_en: Memes?

t_ch: 迷人的反派角色？
t_en: Mesmerizing antagonists?

t_ch: 是「迷因」，对，就是这个。它们还有形体呢。我和其中一个牙尖爪利的迷因生命成了好朋友。它对仙舟持明文化特感兴趣，我就给它起了个仙舟名，它很开心。
t_en: Yes meme, that's it. They even have forms. I'm good friends with a meme with sharp teeth and claws. It's very interested in the Xianzhou Vidyadhara culture. I gave it a Xianzhou name, and it was delighted.

t_ch: 哦，说到这个，我该去找它了。「梗大鬼」脾气火爆，向来没什么耐心。
t_en: Oh, speaking of which, I should go and look for it. Memey the Monster has an explosive temper, and it's never been very patient.

t_ch: 祝你找到真正的糖衣炮弹——我是说，炸弹。拜拜！
t_en: I hope you can find the real sugar-coated cannonball — I mean, bomb. Bye bye!

t_ch: 娃娃体内冒出一缕青烟，随后便不再动弹。看来这也并非真正的虚数中子弹。
t_en: A wisp of green smoke emerges from the doll's body, and then it lies motionless. It seems this isn't a real imaginary neutron bomb either.

t_ch: 你好，亲爱的演员。你可以叫我「导演」娃娃，这是我在片尾字幕常用的署名。
t_en: Hello, lovely actor. You can call me the Director Doll. I often use this name in film credits.

t_ch: 你或许有所不知，其实正是我导演了匹诺康尼的整场闹剧。作为剧目的{M#男一号}{F#女一号}，你现在有什么想法？欢迎畅所欲言……
t_en: If you didn't know, I'm the one who directed the entire farce in Penacony. As the leading {F#lady}{M#man} of the show, what did you think? Feel free to share your thoughts...

t_ch: …好吧，其实根据剧情需要，你现在只能说一句话，所以还是拜托你尽量克制一下畅所欲言的冲动。
t_en: ...Well, actually, due to current plot requirements, you're only allowed to say one line. So, please try and resist the urge to share your thoughts.

t_ch: 我现在只想拆炸弹过剧情。
t_en: Right now, I just want to defuse the bomb and advance the plot.

t_ch: 真的吗？你真的什么都不问吗？好吧，真为你惋惜。
t_en: Really? You really have no questions? Okay then, what a pity.

t_ch: 就是你让星期日做大反派？
t_en: You're the one who made Sunday a villain?

t_ch: 星期日？星期日是谁？
t_en: Sunday? Who's that?

t_ch: 黄泉逃离家族围捕的动作戏呢？
t_en: What about the action scene where Acheron had to escape The Family?

t_ch: 黄泉？黄泉是谁？
t_en: Acheron? Who's that?

t_ch: 知更鸟得知真相的过程去哪了？
t_en: What happened to Robin's journey of uncovering the truth?

t_ch: 知更鸟？知更鸟是谁？
t_en: Robin? Who's that?

t_ch: 你的剧情转场也太多了吧！
t_en: You have way too many storyline transitions!

t_ch: 嗐，怪我，上了假面愚者的当！这剧本出自一位名为「花火大人」的编剧之手。她说她是第一花火大学电影编导系出身，我便与她一拍即合……
t_en: *sigh* It's all my fault for being duped by the Masked Fools! This screenplay was written by someone named "Miss Sparkle." She said she graduated from Sparkle University's Film Directing and Screenwriting program, so we instantly hit it off...

t_ch: …谁知道第一花火大学根本没有什么电影编导系——就连第一花火大学这所高校都不存在！哎，花火。
t_en: ...Who knew that there was no such thing as the "Film Directing and Screenwriting program" at Sparkle University — in fact, Sparkle University isn't even real! Bah, Sparkle.

t_ch: 既然你问完了，我也回答过了…那么根据剧情需要，我将会告诉你这里根本没有炸弹的事实，让你痛悔自己浪费了半天时间听我说废话，然后我们分道扬镳。
t_en: Since you're done asking questions, and I'm done answering... according to the script, I must reveal to you the fact that there's no bomb here at all, thus causing you to want to kick yourself for spending this whole time listening to me prattling on, after which we will part ways.

t_ch: 但别难过！我还为你准备了一份好礼——一枚梦泡——记录了我和一位美丽忆者的生死时速！如果你还没看过，那就快去找爱德华医生吧。
t_en: But don't fret! I've also prepared a gift for you — a dream bubble that has the thrilling life-and-death moments I shared with a beautiful Memokeeper! If you haven't seen it yet, you should hurry up and look for Dr. Edward.

t_ch: 这只完全无害又爱说废话的毛绒娃娃对你深情地鞠了一躬，还送上飞吻，然后一跳一跳地消失在船舱深处。
t_en: The harmless chatterbox plush doll bows affectionately to you, blowing you a kiss before bounding away, vanishing into the depths of the cabin with joyful leaps.

t_ch: 呀哈哈！你找到我了…呃，不是，我是说「谢谢你」啾！
t_en: Yahaha! So you've found me... Uh, no, I meant to say "thank you," tweet!

t_ch: 你是炸弹吗？
t_en: You're a bomb?

t_ch: 什么…你看不出来吗？我是「折纸」娃娃啾！
t_en: What... You can't tell? I'm the Origami Doll, tweet!

t_ch: 不该说的话别乱说。
t_en: Don't speak out of turn.

t_ch: 只、只是串戏了啾！请你千万别告诉别人啾。
t_en: I—I just got the parts mixed up! Please don't tell anyone, tweet.

t_ch: 你现在又是折纸小鸟了？
t_en: So you're the Origami Bird now then?

t_ch: 「折纸小鸟」？你看不出来吗？我是「折纸」娃娃啾！
t_en: Origami Bird? Can't you tell? I'm the Origami Doll, tweet!

t_ch: 其实，像我这样能打的娃娃，在这艘「晖长石号」上还有<unbreak>998</unbreak>个啾。请把它们叫回「黄金花火大树」吧——我会为您准备好谢礼的啾！
t_en: Actually, there are <unbreak>998</unbreak> more dolls that can pack a punch just like me on the Radiant Feldspar, tweet. Please call them back to the Golden Sparkle Tree. I will prepare a worthy gift for your troubles, tweet!

t_ch: 至于你要的虚数中子弹…呃，别打岔，我知道你根本没提过这五个字…那个炸弹不在这里啾！恐怕你只能到别处看看了啾。
t_en: As for that imaginary neutron bomb you want... Uh, don't interrupt me. I know you never mentioned those three words to me before... Well, the bomb isn't here, tweet! I'm afraid you'll have to look elsewhere for it.

t_ch: 古怪的娃娃扇动双马尾消失在了梦境的天空中，这场面看起来可比折纸小鸟惊悚多了。至于「黄金花火大树」…忘了它吧，多半只是花火随手捏的设定罢了。
t_en: The peculiar doll flicks its twin ponytails and vanishes into the sky of dreamscape, a sight much more thrilling than an Origami Bird. As for the "Golden Sparkle Tree"... forget about it, most likely just some random balderdash that Sparkle made up.

t_ch: 这是一只花火造型的「芙莫」娃娃。
t_en: This "Furmur" doll is a Sparkle lookalike.

t_ch: 根据《近现代花火语汇编》一书，「芙莫」一词首次诞生于琥珀<unbreak>2005</unbreak>纪花火星系的花火星，原意为「毛绒绒的」。现在，花火星人多用「芙莫」特指「毛绒娃娃」。
t_en: According to the book "Compilation of Sparkle Vocabulary in Modern and Contemporary Times," the term "furmur" was first coined in <unbreak>2005</unbreak> AE on the Sparkle Planet in the Sparkle star system. Its original meaning is "fluffy." Nowadays, the inhabitants of Sparkle Planet often use "furmur" specifically to refer to "plush dolls."

t_ch: 换句话说，「『芙莫』娃娃」的意思是「毛绒娃娃娃娃」。听起来有点奇怪，但考虑到是花火，倒也合理。
t_en: In other words, "furmur doll" translates to "fluffy doll doll doll doll." It may sound peculiar, but considering its Sparkle origin, it actually makes lots of sense.

t_ch: 「芙莫」娃娃正在盯着你。
t_en: The "Furmur" doll is staring right at you.

t_ch: 我能把你的炸弹拆掉吗？
t_en: Can I dismantle your bomb?

t_ch: 盯回去。
t_en: Stare back.

t_ch: 「芙莫」娃娃还是在盯着你。
t_en: The "Furmur" doll continues to stare right at you.

t_ch: 你说句话，可以吗？
t_en: Can you speak, please!?

t_ch: 继续盯回去。
t_en: Continue to stare back.

t_ch: 「芙莫」娃娃的眼睛眨都不眨。说真的，要是眨了那还怪吓人的。
t_en: The "Furmur" doll's eyes never blink. Honestly, if they did, it would be quite unsettling.

t_ch: 无论如何，它就只是个普通的毛绒玩具而已。你还能指望它说句话不成？
t_en: Anyway, it's just an ordinary plush toy. Can you really expect it to utter a single word?

t_ch: 你要是不说话，那就是默认了。
t_en: If you don't speak, then your silence equates to consent.

t_ch: 持续进行一个盯。
t_en: Keep staring at it.

t_ch: 必须指出的是，这只娃娃没有任何可疑的地方，它由内自外地散发着一个完美「芙莫」娃娃该有的气息，而非一枚虚数中子弹。
t_en: It must be pointed out that this doll has no suspicious features at all. It exudes from within an aura befitting of a "Furmur" doll, not that of an imaginary neutron bomb.

t_ch: 刚才这句话的意思是你不必再骚扰它了——这样你和它看起来都怪可怜的。还是及时终止这场闹剧吧。
t_en: ...which means there is no need to bother it anymore. It makes both you and the doll appear rather pitiful. It's best to promptly put an end to this farce.

t_ch: （还算是有点进展…继续努力吧。）
t_en: (At least I made some progress... Gotta keep working at it.)

t_ch: （时间不多了…希望还来得及。）
t_en: (Not much time left... I hope I make it.)

t_ch: 二十八分四十六秒…二十八分四十五秒……
t_en: Twenty-eight minutes forty-six seconds... Twenty-eight minutes forty-five seconds...

t_ch: …啊，你来了！
t_en: ...Ah, you're here!

t_ch: 炸弹的状况如何了？
t_en: How's the bomb situation?

t_ch: 看来她也给你们发消息了……
t_en: Looks like she also sent you a message...

t_ch: 你也是被假面愚者喊来的吗？
t_en: You were also summoned here by the Masked Fools?

t_ch: 假面愚者…果然是他们干的好事。
t_en: The Masked Fools... So this really was their doing.

t_ch: 花火，吃我一棍！
t_en: Sparkle, eat my bat!

t_ch: 别过来，这颗炸弹很危险！还有…我是真的流萤！
t_en: Don't come close. This bomb is very dangerous! And... I'm the real Firefly!

t_ch: 既然你会出现在这…那我就长话短说吧。大约半小时前，我收到未知发信人的消息，就立刻赶过来了。
t_en: Since you're here... I'll just keep it short. Just over half an hour ago, I received a message from an unknown sender, and rushed here as soon as I could.

t_ch: 二十七分五十二秒…二十七分五十一秒……
t_en: Twenty-seven minutes fifty-two seconds... Twenty-seven minutes fifty-one seconds...

t_ch: 美梦失去了「秩序」的庇护，要是让它在这里爆炸…后果不堪设想。我研究了很久，但这枚炸弹的构造十分特殊，像是被某种莫名的命途力量上了锁……
t_en: The sweet dream has lost the protection of the Order — if it were to blow up here... the consequences would be unfathomable. I've scrutinized it for a long time, but the bomb's design is incredibly unique, as if it's been locked by some mysterious Path force...

t_ch: 除了制造者，恐怕没人能让它停下来了。
t_en: Apart from its creator, I fear no one knows how to deactivate it.

t_ch: 那我们就找到那家伙！
t_en: Then we'll just have to find that person!

t_ch: 很困难。时间有限，她又会易容，而且就算抓到了，她也一定不会乖乖就范……
t_en: It's difficult. Time is running out, and she's a master of disguise. And even if we catch her, she won't come quietly...

t_ch: 别急，还有很多人正在赶来。
t_en: Don't panic. There are still many people on their way.

t_ch: 你是指「钟表匠」的贵宾们吗？可惜…以我的经验，在场的人里应该没人能改变现状。
t_en: You mean the Watchmaker's guests? That's a pity... Based on my experience, I don't think any of them can turn the tide on this situation.

t_ch: 忆者也许能把炸弹转移到没人的地方，但…我在娃娃体内发现了一张纸条。
t_en: The Memokeeper may be able to teleport the bomb to a deserted location, but... I've found a note inside the doll.

t_ch: 不知道她们有什么过节…但这条路也被堵死了。
t_en: I don't know what grudges they have... but this path is a dead end, too.

t_ch: 难道就没别的办法了吗…
t_en: So that's it? There's no other way?

t_ch: 还有其他办法吗…
t_en: Is there another way?

t_ch: 别急，还有很多人正在赶来。
t_en: Don't panic. There are still many people on their way.

t_ch: 你是指「钟表匠」的贵宾们吗？可惜…以我的经验，在场的人里应该没人能改变现状。
t_en: You mean the Watchmaker's guests? That's a pity... Based on my experience, I don't think any of them can turn the tide on this situation.

t_ch: 忆者也许能把炸弹转移到没人的地方，但…我在娃娃体内发现了一张纸条。
t_en: The Memokeeper may be able to teleport the bomb to a deserted location, but... I've found a note inside the doll.

t_ch: 不知道她们有什么过节…但这条路也被堵死了。
t_en: I don't know what grudges they have... but this path is a dead end, too.

t_ch: 那我们就找到那家伙好了。
t_en: Then we'll just have to find that person.

t_ch: 这…应该也办不到。时间有限，她又会易容，而且就算抓到了，她也一定不会乖乖就范……
t_en: That's... probably not possible. Time is running out, and she's a master of disguise. And even if we catch her, she won't come quietly...

t_ch: 难道就没别的办法了吗…
t_en: So that's it? There's no other way?

t_ch: 还有其他办法吗…
t_en: Is there another way?

t_ch: 妈妈…我不想死…
t_en: Mommy... I don't wanna die...

t_ch: 其实…也不是完全没办法。
t_en: Actually... there might be another way.

t_ch: 还记得吗？剧本上说过我会在梦想之地经历三次「死亡」。
t_en: Do you still remember? The script said that I will experience "death" three times in the Land of Dreams.

t_ch: 我想，现在就是「第三次」的前兆了。
t_en: I think this moment heralds the third time.

t_ch: 你打算做什么？
t_en: What's your plan?

t_ch: 都说了别在这种场合回收伏笔！
t_en: Like I said earlier — now's not the time for the plot payoff!

t_ch: 你或许已经知道了，我生来没有做梦的机能，入梦的方式也是星核猎手的特殊手段。这让我能做到一般逐梦客做不到的事。
t_en: You may already know that I have no way of evoking dreams — I employ a Stellaron Hunter's special method in order to enter dreams instead. This allows me to perform feats that typical Dreamchasers can't.

t_ch: 只要承受住忆质重压带来的痛苦，我就可以下潜至美梦之外的原始忆域中，而这将会为「晖长石号」提供一线生机。
t_en: As long as I can bear the pain of the memoria pressure, I'll be able to dive into the primal Memory Zone beyond the dream, and extend a lifeline to the Radiant Feldspar.

t_ch: 我会把这枚炸弹带到{RUBY_B#原始忆域}梦境的深处{RUBY_E#}…至少是尽可能深的无人之地。这样就没人会受伤了。
t_en: I will take this bomb into {RUBY_B#Primordial Memory Zone}the depths of the Dreamscape{RUBY_E#}... as deep as possible, where there are no living souls around. That way, at least no one will get hurt.

t_ch: 那你呢？
t_en: What about you?

t_ch: 真的来得及吗？
t_en: Can you really make it in time?

t_ch: 这事不能交给星核猎手解决。
t_en: This matter can't just be left to the Stellaron Hunters.

t_ch: 别担心，我相信这身{RUBY_B#S.A.M.}火萤装甲{RUBY_E#}足够在倒计时结束前送我到该去的地方…甚至有机会平安返回。
t_en: Don't worry. I believe that this {RUBY_B#S.A.M.}Fyrefly Armor{RUBY_E#} will be enough to take me to where I need to go before the countdown ends... and maybe even make it back safely.

t_ch: 这也是眼下最好，也是最合理的选择了。毕竟这个漫长的故事，理应有一个「圆满」的结局。
t_en: At present, this is our best, and most logical course of action. After all, a long story deserves a happy ending.

t_ch: 分享给你一句话吧，其实是黄泉小姐告诉我的，她说「所谓的不可能之事，只是尚未到来之事」……
t_en: I have some words to share with you, though they were spoken to me by Miss Acheron. She said that "The so-called impossible is merely something that has yet to happen..."

t_ch: 眼下简直有太多不可能发生的事了…但它们真的就绝对不会发生吗？要我说，没准只是这些「不可能」被否定的瞬间还没到来而已。
t_en: At the moment, there are so many things that seem impossible... but are they really never going to happen? Maybe it's just that the moment to disprove these "impossibilities" hasn't arrived yet.

t_ch: 究竟是字面意义的终结，还是有如死亡般的痛苦，或是千钧一发的死境…在既定的终点到来前，它们并无不同，而我依旧能做出许多选择。我也坚信……
t_en: Whether it be a literal ending, suffering akin to death, or a harrowing deathscape... Before the appointed destination arrives, they are all the same, yet I can still make myriad choices. I also firmly believe that...

t_ch: 在做出选择的瞬间，我们心中的「结果」早已有答案了。
t_en: That when that moment arrives for us to make a choice, the answer to our end will already be within our hearts.

t_ch: 并非是命运塑造了我们，而是我们塑造了命运。
t_en: It is not destiny that shapes us, but we who shape destiny.

t_ch: （这艘空艇上的宝贝也不少，收获颇丰……）
t_en: (This airship has quite a few treasures, a bountiful harvest...)

t_ch: （那是…流萤？）
t_en: (Is that... Firefly?)

t_ch: 我来这里时，正巧看见一个孩子捧着它。他说这花是翠丝阿姨准备的，为了「钟表匠」，和他挂念了一辈子的战友。
t_en: When I arrived, I happened to see a child holding it. He said the flowers were prepared by Aunt Jessie, for the Watchmaker, and the war comrade he'd missed his whole life.

t_ch: 米哈伊尔每年都会在这里放上两束花。他离开后，就变成了三束。
t_en: Mikhail would place two bunches of flowers here year after year. And, after he left, it became three.

t_ch: 你的心愿，一直有人记着。
t_en: Your wishes will always be remembered by someone.

t_ch: 现在，匹诺康尼也如你期望的那样，在漫漫长夜后迎来了黎明。前路或许不是一帆风顺，但人们已经做好准备迈向「自由」。
t_en: Now, Penacony as you hoped, has welcomed the dawn after a long, dark night. The path forward may not be a bed of roses, but at least people are prepared to step forth towards freedom.

t_ch: 铁尔南，你可以回家了。
t_en: Tiernan, you can go home now.

t_ch: 而未来的无名客，也准备好启程前往他们的下一站了。
t_en: While the Nameless are also preparing for the next stop of their voyage.

t_ch: 但在离开前，我们还有最后一件事要做。
t_en: But before leaving, we still have one last thing to do.

t_ch: 来和你告别。
t_en: To say goodbye to you.

t_ch: 这是我的荣幸。我经历过太多告别。很高兴，这一次能够笑着和各位说再见。
t_en: It is my honor. I've said many goodbyes. Yet I am glad, that this is the first time I speak those words with a smile.

t_ch: 不过在离开前，你们应该也有不少话想对过去的无名客说吧？
t_en: But before leaving, I'm sure you all have plenty to say to the Nameless of the past?

t_ch: 来和加拉赫告别。
t_en: We've come to say goodbye to Gallagher.

t_ch: 看来你们也发现了，那位虚构史学家的身影从匹诺康尼消失了，就和他来时一样悄无声息。
t_en: Looks like you've also noticed that history fictionologist is nowhere to be found in Penacony, disappearing just as quietly as when he first showed up.

t_ch: 我想，他最后一定到过这里，来和他的无名客朋友道别。同样地，你们应该也有不少话想对他们说吧。
t_en: I believe he must have come here at the end, to bid farewell to his Nameless friends. Similarly, I'm sure you all have plenty you want to say to them.

t_ch: 来和老无名客们告别。
t_en: We've come to say goodbye to the old Nameless.

t_ch: 为逝者的故事画上圆满的句号，就是最好的告慰。去吧，他们都在这里。
t_en: A fitting end to the tale of the departed — one could not ask for a better farewell. Go on, they're all here.

t_ch: 说实话，最初听见列车长的请求时，我还挺吃惊的。
t_en: Honestly, when I heard the conductor's request, I was pretty surprised.

t_ch: 无名客无名客，「开拓」的人做好事从不留名，时间又过去了这么久，要怎么才能在这么大的匹诺康尼找到那三个人的下落呢？
t_en: The Nameless — Those who Trailblaze, doing good deeds but never seeking recognition. After all this time, how would we even find those three people in such a vast place like Penacony?

t_ch: 但现在看来…在「梦想之地」，果然一切都有可能啊。
t_en: But it seems... in the Land of the Dreams, anything is indeed possible.

t_ch: 历史或许不会留下逝者的名字，但群星会见证他们的足迹。
t_en: History may not remember the names of the dead, but the stars will attest to their journeys.

t_ch: 在长夜中到来的第一束光，往往无法照亮什么，因为它转瞬即逝，而黑夜太过漫长。
t_en: The first glimmer of light in the prolonged night often illuminates little, as it is fleeting and the darkness too vast.

t_ch: 但人们会因此记得：如果夜空中总有什么要亮起，那么在第一颗星星落下后…还会有无数的流星划过天际。
t_en: But because of this, people will remember: As long as something shines in the night sky, then when the first star falls... countless more will follow, streaking across the horizon.

t_ch: 举杯。
t_en: A toast.

t_ch: 博雷克林•铁尔南、拉扎莉娜•简•艾丝黛拉，向你们致敬，银轨的开拓者——
t_en: Breukelen Tiernan, Razalina Jane Estella, we raise a toast to you, Trailblazers of the silver rail...

t_ch: ——敬不再沉默的历史、热烈而勇敢的奔赴，和通往群星的旅途。
t_en: ...A toast to history that no longer remains silent, the passionate and courageous pursuit, and a voyage that traverses the stars.

t_ch: 星穹列车的各位准备离开匹诺康尼了么？
t_en: Is the Astral Express ready to depart Penacony?

t_ch: 抱歉，米凯先生，事到如今才来和你们道别。
t_en: Apologies, Mr. Micah, that we are only now bidding you farewell.

t_ch: 哪里的话，你们为「钟表匠」做了太多，我们无以为报，就让我作为流梦礁的代表，再敬各位无名客一杯吧。
t_en: Oh, that's alright. You've all done so much for the Watchmaker, and we are forever indebted. Allow me, as the representative of Dreamflux Reef, to make another toast to all the Nameless.

t_ch: 流梦礁的人们接下来会怎么样？
t_en: What will the people of Dreamflux Reef do now?

t_ch: 多半会继续留在这儿。习惯了清醒的人，一时半会儿也很难适应在黑暗中闭眼的生活。「秩序」褪去后，也总得有人来守望这片原始忆域。
t_en: Many will continue to live here. Those accustomed to being awake will mostly have a hard time getting used to a life of darkness with their eyes closed. Though the Order has faded, there must be someone to watch over this primal Memory Zone.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼的夜很长，还远远未到所有人都能安眠的时候。至于那边的美梦……
t_en: Penacony's nights are long, and there are many who are still far from a good night's sleep. As for the sweet dream over there...

t_ch: 就算没有它，我们不也活到现在了么？
t_en: We're still managing without it, aren't we?

t_ch: 举杯。
t_en: A toast.

t_ch: 米凯，还有流梦礁的各位住民，向你们致意，长梦的守夜人——
t_en: Micah, and residents of Dreamflux Reef, we raise a toast to you, watchers of the long dream...

t_ch: ——敬坚忍的岁月、每个悲伤的夜晚，和终将到来的黎明。
t_en: ...To your tenacity throughout time, to each sorrowful night, and to the dawn that is finally upon us.

t_ch: 这座雕像…上次应该还不在这儿吧。
t_en: That statue... It wasn't here last time.

t_ch: 看来，这就是加拉赫先生留给我们的最后一道「谜题」了。
t_en: Looks like this is the last riddle that Mr. Gallagher left for us.

t_ch: 结果到头来，我们仍未知晓他的真身，甚至无法分辨他是不是一个「活生生的人」。
t_en: In the end, we still failed to figure out his true identity, or if he was even a "living person."

t_ch: 该怎么说，这位大叔真不愧是虚构史学家，我突然想起来，他在影视乐园还说自己只有十三岁呢，不会也意有所指吧？
t_en: What should I say... I mean this guy is definitely a history fictionologist all right. I'm suddenly reminded of the time at the theme park when he said he was only thirteen years old. Could that have meant something, too?

t_ch: 但，也不是所有的事都能用「神秘」来解释……
t_en: Either way, he's an "enigmatic" character for sure.

t_ch: 至少我们在匹诺康尼的这段同行是真的，而他对这片土地的忠诚和热爱…也一定是真实的，对吧？
t_en: At least our journey together in Penacony was real enough, and his loyalty and love for this land... must have been real too, right?

t_ch: 举杯。
t_en: A toast.

t_ch: 「加拉赫」，向你致意，沉眠的猎犬——
t_en: Gallagher, we raise a toast to you, the slumbering Hound...

t_ch: ——敬盛会的邀请函、所有的谎言，和唯一的真相……
t_en: ...To the festival's invitation, to all lies, and the singular truth...

t_ch: …如果还有机会再见，说话别那么谜语人啦。
t_en: ...If we ever meet again, please don't talk in riddles.

t_ch: 结果，最后又绕回了这里。
t_en: In the end, we still came full circle.

t_ch: 这段「开拓」之旅从{NICKNAME}和一位门童的相遇开始，一路兜兜转转，又回到了它最初的起点。
t_en: This Trailblazing expedition started from the moment {NICKNAME} and a bellboy ran into each other. After going on a journey of many twists and turns, they still ended up where they started.

t_ch: 就像时钟的指针转过一圈又一圈，但每一天的开始和结束，永远落在「前进」的十二点。
t_en: Just like a clock's hands that turn round and round, the start and end of each day will always land on twelve o'clock — the advent of time moving forward.

t_ch: 应该不用多说什么了，这一切的故事因你而起，自然也应当以你作结。
t_en: There shouldn't be much left to say. This entire adventure started because of you, and should naturally end with you.

t_ch: 然后，就去翻开它的下一页吧。
t_en: And then, a new page will be turned.

t_ch: 举杯。
t_en: A toast.

t_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔，向你致意，梦想之地的「钟表匠」，星穹列车的无名客——
t_en: Mikhail Char Legwork, we raise a toast to you, Watchmaker of the Land of the Dreams, Nameless of the Astral Express...

t_ch: ——敬匹诺康尼的过去、现在、未来…和稚子至死不渝的梦。
t_en: ...To Penacony's past, present, future... and the child's unwavering dream unto death.

t_ch: 这样一来，我们作为「无名客」的使命就完成了…吧？
t_en: With that, our duty as Nameless should be complete... right?

t_ch: 「开拓」能为人们指明方向，但一个世界的命运，终究要还给属于它的人。
t_en: The Trailblaze can illuminate the way, but ultimately, the future of a world belongs to those who live in it.

t_ch: …总觉得，米哈伊尔先生肯定很想亲眼见证这一天吧。
t_en: ...I still feel that Mr. Mikhail must have really wanted to witness this day himself.

t_ch: 小三月，有什么心事吗？
t_en: What's on your mind, March?

t_ch: 只是有种奇怪的感觉。虽然前几站也有过，但这次格外明显。
t_en: Just a strange feeling. I had it a few stops ago, but it's super strong this time.

t_ch: 不妨说出来吧，或许大家在想同样的事呢？
t_en: Why not talk about it? Maybe everyone's thinking the same thing?

t_ch: …我总会忍不住想，无论是米哈伊尔先生，还是铁尔南先生、拉扎莉娜女士，他们的一生都是很漫长的，肯定经历过好多好多故事吧……
t_en: ...I can't help but think that whether it's Mr. Mikhail, Mr. Tiernan, or Madam Razalina, their lives must have been long, and they must have experienced plenty of stories...

t_ch: 他们也年轻过，和我们一样跌跌撞撞、打打闹闹。伙伴、对手、旅途、冒险、所有难过和开心的回忆…我们习惯的「每一天」，他们也同样经历过。
t_en: They were also young once, stumbling and bumbling around just like us, getting into scraps and mischief, that sort of stuff. Companions, enemies, journeys, adventures, all the sad and happy memories... The "every day" that we're used to, they've lived through them too.

t_ch: 但那些事…全都过去了。
t_en: But those things... are all in the past.

t_ch: 时间会带走一切，珍惜当下。
t_en: Time will wash everything away, so treasure the present.

t_ch: 我知道，但让我很难释怀的…就是「当下」。
t_en: I know, but the thing that I can't get out of my mind... is the present.

t_ch: 我们的故事总有一天也会过去
t_en: One day, our stories will also be a part of the past.

t_ch: 是啊，也许这就是让我很难释怀的地方吧……
t_en: Yeah, maybe that's the precise thing I can't let go of...

t_ch: 你今天好多愁善感…
t_en: You're awfully sentimental today...

t_ch: 本姑娘难得严肃一次，你就配合下嘛！好不好？
t_en: It's rare that I'm this serious, can't you just humor me for once? Sheesh!

t_ch: 我打个比方就好懂了：就像读一本书的时候，如果其中的角色总是遇见坎坷，最后也落得一个充满遗憾的结局，我们的心情总归会有点复杂的，对吧？
t_en: It'll be easier to understand if I use an analogy: Like when you're reading a book, if one of its characters keeps running into obstacles, and experiences an ending full of regrets, we're bound to feel a bit mixed about it, right?

t_ch: 可以理解，但会觉得别扭。
t_en: I could understand, but I'd feel mixed too.

t_ch: 因为我们见过他们生活的点点滴滴，会认为这些人是特别的。
t_en: Because we've seen every nook and cranny of their lives, we see these people as special.

t_ch: 无论如何，这很难令人接受。
t_en: No matter what, that's very hard to accept.

t_ch: 因为我们见过他们生活的点点滴滴，会认为这些人是特别的。
t_en: Because we've seen every nook and cranny of their lives, we see these people as special.

t_ch: 只要不代入，就没有影响。
t_en: You won't be affected as long as you don't live vicariously through them.

t_ch: 对我来说，因为见过他们生活的点点滴滴，会认为这些人是特别的。
t_en: To me, it's because we've seen every nook and cranny of their lives, we see these people as special.

t_ch: 所以就算没那么现实，就算有不合理的地方，也还是会希望结局到来时，这个故事能圆满落幕。可是……
t_en: So even if there are parts of it that aren't really realistic nor logical, we still hope that their story gets a good ending when it comes.

t_ch: 可如果他们…还有我们…其实没那么「特别」呢？
t_en: But what if they... and we... aren't really that special?

t_ch: 当米哈伊尔先生坐在这张椅子上，一天一天等待星穹列车到来的时候，他又是怎么想的？如果在生命的最后，他还能坚定地说出自己从未后悔……
t_en: When Mr. Mikhail sat in this chair, waiting for the Astral Express to arrive every day, what was he thinking? And if, at the end of his life, he could still firmly say he had no regrets...

t_ch: 那现在，我们心里的这种「遗憾」…又是什么呢？
t_en: Then, what is this regret we feel in our hearts right now...?

t_ch: 我想，我们每个人都在寻找这个问题的答案。
t_en: I think, each and every one of us is searching for the answer to this very question.

t_ch: 银河浩瀚，生命渺小，「开拓」的脚步从不停歇，但在宇宙的尺度下，普通人穷尽一生也只能走出一段短短的距离。
t_en: The universe is vast, and our lives are but specks. The Trailblaze never ends, but against the backdrop of the cosmos, the average person's lifelong journey is merely a short stretch.

t_ch: 可就是这么一段极短的路，彼此相连，就能将无数的世界联结在一起。每一个在银轨上留下枕木的人，宇宙或许不记得他们，但我们会记得。
t_en: But it is in this miniscule distance that paths cross, and countless worlds connect. The universe may not remember every person who leaves a tie along the silver rail, but we will.

t_ch: 只要我们还记得，他们的{RUBY_B#「开拓」}故事{RUBY_E#}就还没有结束。
t_en: As long as we remember, their {RUBY_B#The Trailblaze}stories{RUBY_E#} will never end.

t_ch: 而米哈伊尔先生留给我们的，正是在这个问题面前，他给出的解答。它或许并不完美，却能让一位历尽沧桑的老无名客，在人生的最后释然一笑。
t_en: And what Mr. Mikhail has left for us is his answer to this very question. It may not be perfect, but it left a smile on this storied, jaded old Nameless' face at the end of his life.

t_ch: 而它的意义，也将由后来的我们诠释。
t_en: And its meaning will be interpreted by those who come after — us.

t_ch: 答案并不重要，重要的是从别人的答案里，我们自己能得到什么…是吗？
t_en: It's not the answer that's important, but what we can learn from others' answers... right?

t_ch: 这就是「开拓」。
t_en: This is what Trailblazing is.
...Sure.

t_ch: 抱歉抱歉，把气氛搞得这么沉重——丹恒，快说个冷笑话缓解下气氛！
t_en: I'm really sorry for bringing down the vibe! Quick, Dan Heng, tell us a dad joke to lighten the mood!

t_ch: 提前思考永远不是坏事。总有一天，我们都要面对离别。
t_en: It's never a bad thing to reflect. One day, we'll all have to face our own farewells.

t_ch: 但在此之前，我们还有很长的路要一起走。
t_en: But before that, we still have a long way ahead of us together.

t_ch: 是啊，所以眼下最重要的，就是把我们在匹诺康尼的见闻带给列车长。
t_en: So the most important thing right now is to tell the conductor what we saw in Penacony.

t_ch: 然后就做好准备，出发前往「开拓」的下一站吧。
t_en: Then prepare ourselves for our next Trailblazing destination.

t_ch: 还记得刚刚来到匹诺康尼的时候吗？那时谁也想不到，我们的旅途会以这种方式交汇。
t_en: Do you still remember when we first arrived in Penacony? Who would have thought our paths would cross in such a way.

t_ch: 你究竟是什么人？
t_en: Who exactly are you?

t_ch: 这么说来，我甚至都没能做个正式的自我介绍……
t_en: Come to think of it, I didn't even get a chance to formally introduce myself...

t_ch: 简单来说，我是一位受到「虚无」诅咒的自灭者。我的故乡在很久以前遭遇灭顶之灾，整个世界消亡在{RUBY_B#「虚无」IX}祂{RUBY_E#}的倒影下。
t_en: Simply put, I'm a Self-Annihilator who was cursed by the Nihility. My hometown was destroyed a long time ago, and the whole world was erased beneath {RUBY_B#IX, the Nihility}THEIR{RUBY_E#} shadow.

t_ch: 为对抗自灭的结局，我踏上旅途，寻找斩断「虚无」的方法。而漫长的求索过后，我确信自己的目的地就在分割现实与虚无的「黑网」深处。
t_en: In order to fight against the cruel end of self-destruction, I went on a journey, in search of a way to sever the chains of the Nihility. After a long and grueling search, I am convinced that my destination lies within the depths of the Dark Web, where reality and the Nihility are separate.

t_ch: 那里潜藏着名为「第Ⅸ机关」的秘密。终有一日，我会抵达它的所在。
t_en: In there lurks a secret called "Device IX"... One day, I'll reach it.

t_ch: 我们还会再见面吗？
t_en: Will we meet again?

t_ch: 星海浩瀚，就旅途的目的而言，我们恐怕很难再有产生交集的机会了。
t_en: The ocean of stars is vast, and given our destinations, I'm afraid our paths may not cross again.

t_ch: 但「开拓」的前方本就充满未知，而我的刀又恰好锐利到足以斩落命途…只要你我还保有启程的初心，相信总有一日，我们还能够重逢吧。
t_en: But the trailblazing expedition ahead is always full of unknowns, and my blade is sharp enough to sever fate... As long as we maintain our original resolve, I believe there will come a day when we will meet again.

t_ch: 我们曾在哪里见过吗？
t_en: Have we ever met before?

t_ch: 啊…这么说来，我得为自己的失礼道歉。还记得初次见面的时候吗？我曾说过，你让我想起一位故人。
t_en: Ah... In that case, I must apologize for my rudeness. Do you remember when we first met? I once said you reminded me of an acquaintance.

t_ch: 因为「自灭」的诅咒，我的记忆片片剥离，过去变得模糊不堪。在与你走过这么一段路后，我可以确信…彼时模糊的感受只是错觉，我们确实是第一次见面。
t_en: Because of the Self-Annihilator's curse, my memories are stripped away, blurring my past. And after our journey together, what I originally thought were familiar feelings were merely illusions... I believe this was truly our first meeting.

t_ch: 这次是相识，下次就是重逢了。
t_en: With this as our first meeting, we can consider our next meeting to be a reunion.

t_ch: 是啊，很多时候，比起我是怎样的人，我们一同做过怎样的事才更重要。这段故事…我会铭记在心的。
t_en: That's right. What matters more is not who I am but what we have done together. This story... will be forever etched in my heart.

t_ch: 如果…那不是错觉呢？
t_en: What if... that wasn't an illusion?

t_ch: 这是什么意思？你应该不可能和我的过去有关…我的意思是，那里已经无从追溯，只剩一片「虚无」。
t_en: What do you mean? It's improbable that you've crossed paths with my past self... What I mean is, there is nothing left to retrace there — only Nihility.

t_ch: 即便无关，但我们活在当下
t_en: Regardless of the significance, we live in the present

t_ch: 的确，记忆造就了我们，而所有的记忆都源于「现在」，至于未来…它早在其中诞生了，不是么？
t_en: That is true. Our memories make us who we are, and all memories originate from the "now." As for the future... it's already taking root in the now, isn't it?

t_ch: 我可能也见过…和你相似的人。
t_en: I think I might've seen... someone like you.

t_ch: 这样啊…你也有类似的经历吗？那你应当知道，我和你记忆中的她并非同一人。
t_en: I see... You've also had a similar experience? Then you should know that this me and your memory of me are not the same person.

t_ch: <color=#ff3a3e>但有些事是不会改变的。</color>
t_en: <color=#ff3a3e>But some things will never change.</color>

t_ch: …哈哈。
t_en: ...Haha.

t_ch: …很久以前，我也和你一样，拥有无可替代的同伴。我们也曾踏上旅途，在每一个正确的时候，尽己所能，做出最正确的选择。
t_en: ...Long ago, I, too, was like you, with irreplaceable companions. We also embarked on journeys, making the best choices we could whenever we could.

t_ch: 可惜我们没能迎来正确的结果，但此情此景…总会让我觉得他们从未离去。
t_en: Unfortunately, we didn't achieve the outcome we wanted. But moments like this... make me feel like they never even left.

t_ch: 我明白，只是有些怀念。
t_en: I understand. It's a feeling of longing.

t_ch: 嗯，我理解。珍惜此刻的心情，它能让你不忘来时的道路。
t_en: Yes, I understand. Cherish this moment, for it can help you remember the path you've traveled.

t_ch: 我该出发了。
t_en: I should be on my way.

t_ch: 我们都还有各自的路要走，就踏上旅途吧。希望再见时…已是天晴。
t_en: We both still have our own paths to walk. So let's forge ahead. Hopefully, if we meet again... it'll be beneath clear skies.

t_ch: 在这宇宙中，有着无数相似却又相异的世界，和无数相似却又相异的人。
t_en: In this universe, there exist countless worlds that are similar yet different, and countless people who are alike yet distinct.

t_ch: 我也曾踽踽独行，在陌生的星球邂逅「故人」，看见他们的身影和我的过去重叠在一起。
t_en: I, too, have wandered alone, encountering "acquaintances" on strange worlds, seeing their silhouettes overlap with my past.

t_ch: 在你看来，这种「似曾相识」意味着什么？
t_en: In your opinion, what does this "déjà vu" mean?

t_ch: 也许是一种眷恋。
t_en: Perhaps some sort of sentimental attachment.

t_ch: 想回到过去的愿望。
t_en: The wish to return to the past.

t_ch: 对某个人的思念。
t_en: A longing for a certain someone.

t_ch: 眷恋、渴望、思念…它们或许都对，但都不完整。
t_en: Attachment, desire, longing... They may all be right, but they are all incomplete.

t_ch: 我想，它并非外物，而是来自我们自身，是从过去的某个时刻…跨越时间而来的感情。
t_en: I believe it's not something external, but something that originates within us, an emotion that traverses time from a certain moment of our past to reach us.

t_ch: 它也许是温暖、快乐的，也许是痛苦、悲伤的，每当我们回首过去，总会发现身后留下的只是这么一个个微小但难忘的瞬间，和一些在其中永恒不变的事物……
t_en: Perhaps it's a source of warmth and happiness, or maybe it brings pain and sorrow. Each time we reminisce on our past, we always seem to notice a tiny, but unforgettable instant that we left behind us, along with certain other things that remain constant throughout...

t_ch: 那便是我们人生的一段总结，这段时光中你我全部的缩影…我们一路走来的证明。
t_en: That is a summary of our lives, encapsulating everything about us in these moments... proof of our shared path.

t_ch: 我们从中看到自我，然后…得以「存在」。
t_en: Within them, we glimpse our own essence, and thus... we truly "exist."

t_ch: 就像这个故事中的所有人一样，为了寄托他们生命意义的事物，热烈而勇敢地奔赴在命运的道路上。
t_en: Just like everyone in this story, hurtling onwards along the path of destiny with passion and courage, for the things that breathe meaning into their lives.

t_ch: 义无反顾地踏上旅途吧，走在「开拓进行时」的无名客。
t_en: Set forth on your voyage without hesitation, Nameless, on the path of the Trailblaze.

t_ch: 就算结局早已注定，那也无妨，人改变不了的事太多。
t_en: Even if the ending has been predetermined, that's fine. There are countless things that humans cannot change.

t_ch: 但在此之前，在走向结局的路上，我们能做的事同样很多。
t_en: But before that, on the road towards the end, there are still many things that we can do.

t_ch: 而「结局」…也会因此展现截然不同的意义。
t_en: And because of this, the "end" will thus reveal a completely different meaning.

t_ch: 这便是「旅途」的含义。
t_en: This is the meaning of "journey."

t_ch: 那美丽的事物从前如此，现在亦然，而我同样相信……
t_en: All those things, beautiful before, are still so now. And I believe...

t_ch: 它会在「虚无」的尽头依旧盛放，直到我们在阳光下重逢。
t_en: It will still bloom at the end of the Nihility, until we meet again beneath the sun's rays.

t_ch: 呜呜…帕呜……
t_en: *sob*... *sniff*...

t_ch: 好啦好啦…帕姆，打起精神来！别伤心，别哭啦……
t_en: There there... Chin up, Pom-Pom! Don't be sad. Don't cry...

t_ch: 你安慰人的方法还真古朴。
t_en: Wow, your method of consolation is truly unsophisticated.

t_ch: …总比在旁边看戏强吧！
t_en: ...Still better than just standing there like a scarecrow!

t_ch: 发生什么事了？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 哎，你终于回来啦！
t_en: Hey, you're finally back!

t_ch: 把这次的冒险经历告诉帕姆以后，它就突然开始哭了…我从来没见过帕姆这么伤心……
t_en: We told Pom-Pom all about our adventure, and... they suddenly started crying... I've never seen Pom-Pom so sad before...

t_ch: 三月又欺负帕姆了？
t_en: Is March picking on Pom-Pom again?

t_ch: 怎么可能！别说风凉话了，你也来安慰安慰帕姆呀！
t_en: How could you say that! Don't be so mean. And while you're here, why don't you help me comfort Pom-Pom!

t_ch: 把这次的冒险经历告诉帕姆以后，它就突然开始哭了…我从来没见过帕姆这么伤心……
t_en: We told Pom-Pom all about our adventure, and... they suddenly started crying... I've never seen Pom-Pom so sad before...

t_ch: 呜呜…列车长…列车长才不会哭！才没有…没有在伤心！
t_en: *sob*... The con... conductor never cries! Pom-Pom... is never sad!

t_ch: 帕姆只是…只是……
t_en: Pom-Pom is just... just...

t_ch: …只是在生气帕！对，生气！
t_en: ...Just angry. Yeah, angry!

t_ch: 每次…每次不管列车停靠在哪，你们总是要搞得天翻地覆帕！帕姆预先计划好的发车时间，根本没有乘客会遵守！再这样下去，列车的燃料就要耗光了帕！
t_en: No matter where the Express stops, you lot always manage to cause chaos! My well-thought-out timetable? Completely ignored! If you carry on like this, the Express is going to run out of fuel!

t_ch: 没错，帕姆只是在生气…才不是因为米沙、铁尔南、拉扎莉娜他们——
t_en: That's right. Pom-Pom is just angry... It's not because of Misha, Tiernan, Razalina and the rest...

t_ch: ——呜呜呜哇！
t_en: ...Bwaahhh!

t_ch: 没关系，尽情哭出来吧，帕姆。
t_en: It's okay. Pom-Pom, just let it all out.

t_ch: 大家，能先去隔壁车厢休息一下吗？别担心，这边有我陪着就好。
t_en: Everyone, could you all take a break in the next car? Don't worry, I'll stay here with Pom-Pom.

t_ch: 但是……
t_en: But...

t_ch: 走吧，三月。
t_en: Let's go, March.

t_ch: 没想到帕姆的反应会那么大……
t_en: I never expected Pom-Pom would be so distraught...

t_ch: 那三位「无名客」肯定是它非常重要的伙伴。
t_en: Those three Nameless must have meant a lot to Pom-Pom.

t_ch: 没有人知道帕姆是什么时候登上列车的，但可以想见的是，它这一路上一定经历了许多我们难以想象的相见和离别。
t_en: No one knows exactly when Pom-Pom boarded the Express, but one can surmise that their journey has been filled with many hellos and goodbyes, probably more than we can imagine.

t_ch: 它能像这样大哭一场，在我看来反而是件好事。这证明帕姆没有被漫长的时光磨耗，它仍然无比珍视每一位登上过列车的无名客、珍视每段和他们共同经历的旅程。
t_en: The fact that they're crying so hard is probably a good sign. It proves that Pom-Pom's emotions haven't become dulled by the grind of time — they still deeply cherish every Nameless who has boarded the Express and value every journey shared with them.

t_ch: 安抚的工作就交给姬子吧，毕竟这列车上没人比她更会「开导」别人了。
t_en: Leave it to Himeko. When it comes to comforting, there's no one better on the Express.

t_ch: 帕姆说「燃料」就快耗光了…
t_en: Pom-Pom said we might run out of fuel...

t_ch: 虽然帕姆当时更多是在宣泄情绪…但它说的也确实没错。
t_en: Well, they were a little emotional at the time, but... I'm afraid that's not out of the question.

t_ch: 以{NICKNAME}加入为节点，列车在之后的每一站都耗费了比预期更久的时间。为了确保全员齐整，帕姆不得不一再推迟跃迁时刻表。
t_en: Since {NICKNAME} joined us, the Express has stayed longer than anticipated at every stop along the way. And to ensure that everyone always makes it back on board, Pom-Pom has had no choice but to delay the warp jump schedule.

t_ch: 原来如此…怪不得常听到帕姆在走廊上焦虑踱步。
t_en: I see... No wonder I can regularly hear Pom-Pom pacing anxiously up and down the corridor.

t_ch: 原来列车长也一直在默默为我们付出啊……
t_en: Turns out, Pom-Pom's been silently putting in a lot of work for us, wow...

t_ch: 和一般的载具不同，星穹列车会将一次次的「开拓」转化成维持运行的能源。理想状态下，只要开拓之旅不曾间断，列车便能获得源源不断的动力，如永动机一般持续前行。
t_en: Different from typical vehicles, the Astral Express converts every "Trailblaze" into the energy it needs to run. Ideally, as long as trailblazing expeditions continue without interruption, the Express will receive a constant flow of energy, much like a perpetual motion machine.

t_ch: 但因为此前的遭遇…燃料的消耗比预想中更迅速，再进行两次跃迁可能就是极限了。
t_en: But because of our previous encounters... fuel is being used up much faster than expected. We can probably only pull off two more warp jumps at most.

t_ch: 两次…那岂不是已经很危险了？噫，我可不想又变回在太空中漂泊的冰块啊！
t_en: Only two more... Isn't that super risky? Ahh! I don't wanna become an ice cube floating around in space again!

t_ch: 是「美少女冰块」。
t_en: At least you'll be an adorable ice cube.

t_ch: 你这么一说，好像场面真就没那么凄凉啦？
t_en: When you put it like that, it doesn't actually sound too bad.

t_ch: 噫，我可不想又变回在太空中漂泊的*美少女冰块*啊！
t_en: But I don't even wanna become an adorable ice cube floating around in space again!

t_ch: 落叶总要归根。
t_en: Falling leaves must return to their roots.

t_ch: …那要不，我们再把你塞回空间站的电脑里？
t_en: ...How about we shove you back in the space station's computer, then?

t_ch: 也就是说，在选择下一个目的地时必须考虑这点。
t_en: Which also means that we must prudently consider our next destination.

t_ch: 没错。我已经查看过星图了，距离我们较近的世界有<color=#dbc291ff>「海洋星球」露莎卡星</color>和<color=#dbc291ff>「玛瑙世界」梅露丝坦因</color>。
t_en: Yes. I've already checked the astral charts — the two nearest worlds to us are <color=#dbc291ff>the oceanic planet of Lushaka</color> and <color=#dbc291ff>the Agate World, Melustanin</color>.

t_ch: 至于选择其中哪个作为我们的目的地，还是得经过投票……
t_en: As for which one we're headed to, that still requires a vote...

t_ch: …或者，各位也可以听听我的提议。
t_en: ...Or, perhaps you might consider a suggestion.

t_ch: 又见面了，各位。
t_en: Everyone, we meet again.

t_ch: 是、是你？！你怎么会从我的房间里……
t_en: I—It's you? Why were you just in my room...

t_ch: 很可爱的房间呢，三月小姐——和你本人一样。
t_en: It's a very cute room, Miss March... just like you.

t_ch: 黑天鹅，你怎么在这儿？
t_en: Black Swan, what are you doing here?

t_ch: 我就说道别时少了个人…
t_en: I knew we were missing someone...

t_ch: 忆者…姑且不论你是如何避开耳目登上列车的，你刚才所说的「提议」……
t_en: Memokeeper... Let's put aside how you managed to sneak past everyone and board the Express for now — you mentioned a suggestion...

t_ch: 关于列车获得「燃料」的方式，我不小心都听到了呢。
t_en: I accidentally overheard how the Express obtains fuel.

t_ch: 我原本只是想来和各位聊聊天，看看我们之间是否可能达成合作——现在看来，我的提议很可能也是各位的救命稻草哦。
t_en: I just wanted to chat with everyone to see if we could work together, but now, it appears my suggestion could be the very lifeline that saves everyone.

t_ch: 有话直说吧。取决于内容，我们也可能会请你下车。
t_en: Please speak candidly. Depending on what you say, we could very well ask you to disembark.

t_ch: 「不朽」的后人吗…还真是一条有魅力的小龙呢，尤其是你那段浑浊不清的记忆。
t_en: Ah, the Permanence's descendant... What a charming little dragon, especially with those mired memories of yours.

t_ch: 不说题外话了。如果星穹列车现在急需一趟特别的开拓之旅来为引擎补充「动力」——
t_en: But I digress. If the Astral Express is in urgent need of a special trailblazing expedition to recharge its engine...

t_ch: 各位可曾想过？如果你们此行的目的地是连大名鼎鼎的阿基维利都未曾抵达过的世界……
t_en: Have you all considered this? Perhaps your destination could be a world that even the renowned Akivili never reached...

t_ch: 如果你们能在宇宙中铺下一段崭新的银轨，那列车恐怕就再也不用为能源发愁了。
t_en: Should you be able to lay down a new stretch of silver rail, the Express may never have to worry about energy ever again.

t_ch: 开拓连阿基维利都没去过的世界…这真的能做到吗？
t_en: Trailblazing to a world that even Akivili has never been to... Is that possible?

t_ch: 继续说吧，忆者。你口中的目的地，那是一个怎样的世界？
t_en: Continue, Memokeeper. This destination of which you speak, what sort of world is it?

t_ch: 一个宇宙中绝大多数人都不知道其存在的世界……
t_en: A world that many across the universe don't even know exists...

t_ch: 一个难以从外部被观测到，只能被忆庭之镜照映出来的世界……
t_en: A world hidden away from outside observation, its presence only revealed by the light from the mirror of the Garden of Recollection...

t_ch: 一个被三重命途缠裹绑缚，命运未卜的世界……
t_en: A world fettered by three Paths, its destiny hanging in the balance...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>「永恒之地，翁法罗斯」。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>The Eternal Land, Amphoreus.</color>

t_ch: 希望我没有来晚了，孩子。
t_en: I hope I'm not too late, child.

t_ch: …我没想到会是你。
t_en: ...I wasn't expecting it to be you.

t_ch: 你不知道家族设下了多少道岗哨，要把你从这里带出去得有多难？
t_en: Don't you know how many sentry posts The Family has built, and how hard it is to get you out of here?

t_ch: 看来我的时候到了？
t_en: Looks like my time's up?

t_ch: 我不太明白你的意思，你想说…什么「时候」？
t_en: What do you mean... What "time?"

t_ch: 谈判、审讯，或者一场彻头彻尾的私刑…我的下场完全取决于你，慈玉女士。
t_en: Negotiation, interrogation, or death... My fate lies entirely in your hands, Lady Bonajade.

t_ch: 事到如今，何必再假情假意地赏给将死之人开口的机会？
t_en: The dance is done. Why bother with the compassionate pretense and give someone who's about to die the chance to talk?

t_ch: 即便从神坛跌落，你这副姿态依旧甚好。看到你这么有活力，我很高兴。
t_en: Despite your fall from grace, you still look well. I'm very glad to see that you're so full of verve.

t_ch: 别再用哑谜辱没我的尊严。你专程来见我，就只是为了满足自己恶毒的虚荣心么？
t_en: Do not insult my pride with half-veiled sarcasm. Have you specially come to see me just to sate your vile vanity?

t_ch: 当然不。我来是为了满足你妹妹的愿望，提供一份绝对优渥的交易——只看你愿不愿意接受而已。
t_en: Of course not. I merely came to fulfill your younger sister's wishes. To offer you a generous trade — that is, if you're willing to accept.

t_ch: 知更鸟…？
t_en: Robin...?

t_ch: 「建造一片每个人都能获得安宁的真正乐园」，这是你们兄妹两人的约定，对吧？
t_en: To build a true haven where everyone can attain peace — that's the oath between you siblings, isn't it?

t_ch: 如果我说你还有机会实现这份约定…你会愿意和我谈谈么？
t_en: If I told you there was still a chance to realize this vow... would you be willing to talk to me then?

t_ch: 我知道这个问题很沉重，所以，不需要现在就答应我。
t_en: I recognize the gravity of this question, which is why you don't have to answer me right now.

t_ch: 走吧，你自由了，妄图超越本分的神选者。把自己的羽翼折断，到人间去，走在大地上，看看这人世真正的模样。
t_en: Go now. You are free — O chosen one, who dared to exceed his bounds. Sever your wings, descend to the mortal realm, and walk their lands. See what this world is truly like.

t_ch: 我不会接受你们的施舍。
t_en: I will not accept your charity.

t_ch: 我说过，这是一份交易，而且不需要你立刻回应。收获不在一朝一夕，我最擅长的事情就是等待。美梦还在继续，夜晚还长，你有的是时间慢慢考虑。
t_en: As I mentioned earlier: It's a trade, and you don't have to give me an answer right now. Rewards are not reaped in a day, and if there's one thing I'm best at, it's waiting. The sweet dream still continues, and the night is still long — you have plenty of time to contemplate your answer.

t_ch: 啊，临别前再附赠你一句话吧——权当是过来人的忠告……
t_en: Ah, a word of advice for you before we part ways — a word of warning from someone who's been in your shoes before...

t_ch: 人生苦短，良机莫失。
t_en: Life is too short to miss out on golden opportunities.

t_ch: （该回列车了…或者，我还可以和黄泉作最后的道别。）
t_en: (I should get back to the Express... Or, maybe I could say my final goodbye to Acheron.)

t_ch: 与众无名客道别
t_en: Bid farewell to the Nameless

t_ch: 等待谐乐大典开始
t_en: Wait for the Charmony Festival to start

t_ch: 与加拉赫道别
t_en: Bid farewell to Gallagher

t_ch: 与米哈伊尔道别
t_en: Bid farewell to Mikhail

t_ch: 请跟随引导，稍作等候。
t_en: Please follow the guide and wait for a while.

t_ch: 不会有人在船上安了炸弹吧…
t_en: No way someone could have planted a bomb on this ship, right...?

t_ch: 怎么可能？相信猎犬。
t_en: How is that even possible? Have faith in those Bloodhounds.

t_ch: 老人和小孩优先。
t_en: The elderly and children go first.

t_ch: 谢谢，谢谢。
t_en: Thank you. Thank you.

t_ch: 你说谁是小孩？
t_en: Hey, who are you calling a child?

t_ch: 不要慌，这只是……
t_en: Calm down, this is just...

t_ch: …技术性调整。
t_en: ...A technical correction.

t_ch: 你们公司又在搞什么鬼？！
t_en: What the heck is wrong with your company this time!?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你可算来啦！都等你好半天了。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you're finally here! We've been waiting for so long.

t_ch: 也就五分钟而已。
t_en: It's only been five minutes.

t_ch: 既然大家都到齐了，咱们就进入正题吧。
t_en: Since everyone's here, let's get down to business.

t_ch: 就在刚才，星穹列车收到了来自「盛会之星」的邀请函……
t_en: The Astral Express just received an invitation to the Planet of Festivities...

t_ch: 等等！怎么感觉这个桥段似曾相识？！
t_en: Hang on! Why am I getting a sense of deja vu?

t_ch: 又要来场谐乐大典？
t_en: Is it going to be a repeat of the Charmony Festival?

t_ch: 就算是「盛会之星」也不能天天办盛会吧！
t_en: Even the Planet of Festivities can't possibly hold a festival every day!

t_ch: 又要去找「钟表匠」的遗产？
t_en: Are we going to look for the Watchmaker's Legacy again?

t_ch: 那位前辈也没留下这么多遗产吧……
t_en: I don't think he had that many legacies to leave behind...

t_ch: 我们不会还在梦里吧…
t_en: Are we all still in a dream?

t_ch: 别啊，同一种套路别来第二遍了！
t_en: Please don't! We don't need to go through that again!

t_ch: 别紧张，这次列车收到的是一场普通邀约，来自匹诺康尼的学府「折纸大学」。
t_en: Don't worry — the Express received a normal invitation this time. It's from Penacony's "Paperfold University".

t_ch: 校方打算在「太阳的时刻」筹办一场有关「开拓」的学术研讨会，邀请星穹列车作为嘉宾出席。
t_en: The university plans to hold an academic conference on the topic of "Trailblazing" in the Moment of Sol. The Astral Express has been invited as guests to the conference.

t_ch: 学术研讨…是要坐在大讲堂里，对着几百页幻灯片聊上好几个系统时？
t_en: An academic conference... You mean we'll have to sit in an auditorium and spend several system hours talking about a few hundred slides' worth of scholarly discourse?

t_ch: 放心，这种「枯燥」的活动就交给大人来处理吧。我和瓦尔特会应邀参与研讨，而你们三人……
t_en: Don't worry, you can leave such "boring" events to the grown-ups. I will attend the conference with Welt. As for the three of you...

t_ch: 如果有兴趣，大家可以以「旁听生」的身份进入大学。
t_en: If you are interested, you can enter the university as guest students.

t_ch: 旁听生？
t_en: Guest students?

t_ch: 时逢开学季，折纸大学正在筹办「彩梦校庆」，既是献给新生的开学礼物，也是在校生一年一度的狂欢庆典。
t_en: Well, it happens to be the start of the academic term, and Paperfold University is preparing for its Dreamlight Anniversary. These annual festivities serve as both a welcoming gift for new students, and a chance to celebrate for those already enrolled.

t_ch: 都是年轻人，你们不妨和新生一同入学，在校园里随便走走，体验下学生生活。
t_en: Why don't you youngsters join the new students? You could explore the campus, experience student life...

t_ch: 要、要读书吗……
t_en: D—Do we have to study?

t_ch: …真是太棒了！青春活泼的美少女在校园里挥洒人生，这种情节简直是为本姑娘量身定制的嘛！
t_en: ...That's great! A young cute girl full of energy living her best life on campus? That experience is practically made for me!

t_ch: 据说「折纸大学」是寰宇皆知的顶尖艺术学府，我也很想见识一下。
t_en: I hear that Paperfold University is well-known throughout the universe as a top arts school. I would love to check it out too.

t_ch: 校园生活，我来了！
t_en: Campus life, here I come!

t_ch: 校园果然对年轻人充满吸引力啊。
t_en: Campus life...

t_ch: 太好了，有文凭了！
t_en: Awesome! I'll finally have some academic credentials!

t_ch: 呃，你的关注点好现实哦……
t_en: That's a really practical way of looking at it...

t_ch: 恐怕要让你失望了，折纸大学不会为旁听生颁发凭证。但我相信，你会收获远比证书更有价值的事物。
t_en: Sorry to disappoint, but Paperfold University doesn't issue certificates to guest students. However, I believe you'll gain something far more valuable than a certificate.

t_ch: 救命，我不想上学！
t_en: Help! I don't want to go to school!

t_ch: 不用担心，旁听生不会有繁重的学业。师生们也都在忙着筹备庆典，就当是参加一场派对吧。
t_en: Haha, don't worry — guest students don't get a lot of homework. Besides, all of the professors and students are busy preparing for the festivities. Just... treat it as a party.

t_ch: 不用考试和写作业的校园生活——简直就是天堂嘛！
t_en: Campus life without homework or examinations? That sounds like paradise!

t_ch: 看来大家都已经等不及了，那就这么定了。我会向折纸大学递交入学材料，准备好了就出发，一定要玩得开心啊。
t_en: Looks like everyone can't wait. Let's go ahead with that plan, then. I will submit the admission documents to Paperfold University. We'll leave once everyone is ready. Let's all have some fun.

t_ch: 校园，真是个令人怀念的词啊。
t_en: "Campus..." That word sure brings back a lot of memories.

t_ch: 这么说来，记得你在故乡也有过一段任教经历，想起什么有趣的事了吗？
t_en: Now that you mention it, I recall that you had a teaching stint back home. Any delightful stories that come to mind?

t_ch: 哈哈，那可太多了，准备入学材料时慢慢和你说吧。
t_en: Haha, too many to count. Then again, these admission documents will take us a while to complete — I'll see if I can dig up any stories for you.

t_ch: 选修课、社团、校园派对…本姑娘全都要体验一遍！
t_en: Electives, extracurricular activities, campus parties... I want to try them all!

t_ch: 带带我。
t_en: Take me along.

t_ch: 当然啦，现在开始咱们就是同学了。
t_en: Of course. We're schoolmates now!

t_ch: 可能没你想的那么美好。
t_en: It might not be as fun as you're imagining.

t_ch: 瞧你紧张的，上个学而已，能坏到哪去？
t_en: Ooh, is someone having a case of nerves? We're just going to school. How bad can it be?

t_ch: 其实，我也没上过学。
t_en: Actually, I've never been to school before.

t_ch: 唉，列车上就属咱俩的教育经历有些扑朔迷离…哦不对，还有帕姆。
t_en: You and I are the only ones with ambiguous educational qualifications on the Express... Oh wait, there's Pom-Pom too.

t_ch: 安下心，好好享受校园生活吧。{NICKNAME}同学，请多指教！
t_en: Never mind. Let's just relax and enjoy ourselves on campus. I'll be in your care now, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 作为匹诺康尼的最高学府，折纸大学严苛的考核与昂贵的学费让无数考生望而却步，能进入其中就读的不是声名显赫的富商，就是天赋过人的才子。
t_en: As the top university in Penacony, the strict examinations and expensive tuition have deterred many potential candidates. The people who are admitted to the school are either well-known wealthy merchants or exceptionally talented geniuses.

t_ch: 因此，听说校园风气也非常「匹诺康尼」，真让人好奇。
t_en: Because of this, I hear that the culture on campus has a truly Penaconian flavor. How intriguing.

t_ch: 舒适的沙发，躺着就会感到昏昏欲睡，希望这次旅途不会把你送进一场奇怪的梦里。
t_en: A comfy couch that makes you drowsy as soon as you lie down on it — let's just hope this trip doesn't send you into another strange dream.

t_ch: 准备出发。
t_en: Ready to go.

t_ch: 再和大家聊聊。
t_en: Talk with the others a little longer.

t_ch: 蕉，蕉，蕉，蕉——
t_en: Ba, na, na, na~

t_ch: （什么鬼动静？）
t_en: (What is that ruckus?)

t_ch: 蕉，蕉，蕉，蕉——
t_en: Ba, na, na, na~

t_ch: 古怪的声音在耳边萦绕，但睡意胜过了噪音的干扰，你的眼皮不为所动……
t_en: The strange sounds lingered by your ears, but your drowsiness overcame the noise, and your eyelids remained unmoved...

t_ch: 前往匹诺康尼的途中，你不出意外地打了个盹儿……
t_en: On your way to Penacony, you unsurprisingly dozed off...

t_ch: 我们到啦。{NICKNAME}，你怎么每次跃迁都要犯瞌睡？叫都叫不醒的。
t_en: We've arrived. {NICKNAME}, why do you always fall asleep when we're warping? I couldn't even wake you up.

t_ch: 睡少了，太困。
t_en: I'm tired. Not enough sleep.

t_ch: 你呀，大晚上不睡觉都干什么去了？
t_en: What have you been doing at night instead of sleeping?

t_ch: 原来鬼动静是你？
t_en: Oh, so that ruckus was you?

t_ch: 什么鬼动静！我喊你的时候很温柔的，好不好？
t_en: What do you mean ruckus!? I was really gentle when trying to wake you up.

t_ch: 唉，我怎么睡得着啊！
t_en: Uh, what do you mean I fell asleep!?

t_ch: 算了，待会儿上课时可别再迷糊啦，不然一觉就把你送去流梦礁了。
t_en: Never mind. Just don't look so lost when we're in class later, or you might just sleep yourself to Dreamflux Reef.

t_ch: 好了，我们去办理入住吧。
t_en: Alright, let's go check in.

t_ch: 姬子和杨叔还得参加应酬，大人们可真辛苦啊。
t_en: Himeko and Mr. Yang still have to participate in social networking. Being an adult sure is hard.

t_ch: 欢迎光临「白日梦」酒店，尊敬的股东们。
t_en: Welcome to The Reverie, distinguished shareholders.

t_ch: 啊——差点忘了，我们现在是匹诺康尼的股东了！
t_en: Oh, I almost forgot that we're Penacony's shareholders!

t_ch: 听闻各位股东重回匹诺康尼，酒店也早早腾出了六间铂金客房，随时可以入住。
t_en: Upon hearing the news that you were returning to Penacony, the hotel prepared six Platinum Guest Rooms for your stay. They are ready for you anytime.

t_ch: 六间？
t_en: Six?

t_ch: 是的，除了在场三位、姬子小姐、瓦尔特先生外，还有三天前已经入住的帕姆先生。
t_en: That's right. Besides the three of you, Miss Himeko, and Mr. Yang... Pom-Pom had already checked in three days ago.

t_ch: 帕姆？
t_en: Pom-Pom?

t_ch: 帕姆？你在说什么呀……
t_en: Pom-Pom? What are you talking about?

t_ch: …我明白了。感谢家族招待，请为我们三人办理入住吧。
t_en: ...I see. We thank The Family for their gracious hospitality. Please help us with the check-in.

t_ch: 当然。各位的「梦境护照」已经激活，希望匹诺康尼能再次为大家带来一段愉快的时光！
t_en: Of course. Your Dreamscape Passes have been activated. I hope that you have another enjoyable stay in Penacony.

t_ch: 上次的时光可不怎么愉快呀……
t_en: We didn't have an enjoyable stay the last time, though...

t_ch: 抱歉，刚才打断了你们。
t_en: Apologies for interrupting you earlier.

t_ch: 丹恒，你是不是知道什么？前台说帕姆也入住了酒店是怎么回事？
t_en: Dan Heng, do you know something that we don't? Why did the front desk mention that Pom-Pom is staying at the hotel too?

t_ch: 如果我没猜错，这位「冒名顶替」的来访者…多半是波提欧。
t_en: If my guess is correct, it's probably Boothill checking in under Pom-Pom's name.

t_ch: 波提欧？
t_en: Boothill?

t_ch: 那个巡海游侠？
t_en: That Galaxy Ranger?

t_ch: 宝了个贝的，是他啊！
t_en: You mean Mr. Holy Forkeroni!?

t_ch: 嗯。先前在匹诺康尼一起行动时，他也借用过帕姆的名号。我只能想到他。
t_en: Yes. When we worked together in Penacony the last time... he used Pom-Pom's identity then, too. He's the only one I can think of who would do that.

t_ch: 波提欧？他来做什么。
t_en: Boothill? What is he doing here?

t_ch: 不知道。巡海游侠一向独来独往，但波提欧应该不会做出有害列车的行为。
t_en: I don't know. Galaxy Rangers always work alone, but I don't think Boothill will do anything that is detrimental to the Express.

t_ch: 既然这位「帕姆」三天前就已入住，我们多半能在梦里找到他。到时再详细询问吧。
t_en: Since "Pom-Pom" checked in three days ago, we can probably find him in the dream. We'll ask him more about it then.

t_ch: 那就出发吧。折纸大学位于「太阳的时刻」，酒店已经将入梦地点设置在校园附近啦，咱们校门口集合！
t_en: Let's get going then. Paperfold University is located in the Moment of Sol. The hotel has set the entrance to the dream near the school, so let's meet at the gates!

t_ch: （我的房间还是上次那间…准备入梦吧。）
t_en: (I'm staying in the same room as the last time I was here... Time to enter the dream.)

t_ch: 搞快点！听人说大股东们已经到大堂了，千万不能惊动了人家！
t_en: Hurry up! I hear that the major shareholders have already arrived at the lobby. Don't alarm them!

t_ch: 我知道啊！但那家伙明明就是往这个方向跑的，怎么一下子就没影了……
t_en: I know! But that fella was running in this direction. How did he disappear so quickly...?

t_ch: 嘘，有人闯进来了——你是什么人？
t_en: Shh! Someone else is here. Who are you?

t_ch: 我是星穹列车的无名客。
t_en: I'm one of the Nameless on the Astral Express.

t_ch: 星什么车？别给我推销，我就这点儿工资，买不起车！
t_en: The what Express now? Don't try to sell me anything. My miserable pay can't afford to buy an Express of any sorts!

t_ch: 我是盛会海选的巨星。
t_en: I'm the Superstar from the Festivity Auditions.

t_ch: 盛什么选？抱歉，哥们我闲时都只顾沉淀，从来不看娱乐综艺！
t_en: The what Auditions? Sorry, I usually just relax in my free time and never watch variety shows!

t_ch: 我就是「大人物」。
t_en: I'm a VIP.

t_ch: 得了吧，{F#姐们}{M#哥们}，来这的都说自己是大人物，我都听麻了。
t_en: Oh, cut it out. Everyone who comes here calls themselves a VIP. It doesn't mean anything to me by this point.

t_ch: 不管你是谁，猎犬家系正在这查案呢，麻烦绕个道……
t_en: I don't care who you are. The Bloodhound Family is investigating a case here. Please make way.

t_ch: 喂！灰头发，黄眼睛，衣服上挂着几条莫名其妙的带子…{F#她}{M#他}好像就是那位大股东！
t_en: Wait a second! Gray hair, yellow eyes, ribbons that serve no purpose on {F#her}{M#his} clothes... I think {F#she}{M#he}'s the major shareholder you were talking about!

t_ch: 什么？！我的神主，你早说啊！
t_en: What!? For the love of... Couldn't you have said that earlier!?

t_ch: ——哎呀，贵宾，我刚说话有点大声了，您多见谅！猎犬们只是在进行例行检查，现在客房设施一切正常，您尽管放心入住。
t_en: Ahem, I beg your pardon, esteemed guest. I was a bit brusque earlier. The Bloodhounds are just carrying out a routine inspection. All guest rooms and facilities are in good order, so please be at ease and enjoy your stay.

t_ch: 可是犯人……
t_en: But... the suspect...

t_ch: 犯人？
t_en: Suspect?

t_ch: 你——啊对对对，烦人，确实烦人！但这些流程都是为了宾客们的安全，猎犬从不嫌麻烦！我们这就离开，您好好休息啊。
t_en: You...! Sus... sustainable! Ah, yes, sustainable! We know frequent inspections may not seem sustainable to some, but these procedures ensure a safe and secure environment for guests, so the Bloodhounds are more than happy to do so. We'll take our leave for now. Please have a good rest!

t_ch: 那个…您一定要在梦中注意安全，务必小心……
t_en: Um... Please stay safe, and be careful in the dream...

t_ch: 差不多得了，赶紧跟我走！
t_en: Alright, that's enough. Come on, let's go!

t_ch: （这都什么跟什么…要不在入梦前再检查一下？）
t_en: (I'm confused... Maybe I should look around before I enter the dream?)

t_ch: （箱子打不开，总觉得心里有个疙瘩……）
t_en: (I can't open the box... Something just doesn't feel right...)

t_ch: （但也不能让三月七和丹恒等太久，还是先睡吧。）
t_en: (But I can't keep March 7th and Dan Heng waiting. Never mind, I'll head to sleep for now.)

t_ch: 专注呼吸，想象一座乐园…想象…一片……
t_en: Focus on your breathing and imagine you're in paradise... Imagine... an... island...

t_ch: 一片、一片、一片、一片、一片……
t_en: An... island... island... island... island... island...

t_ch: 一片——长满香蕉的小岛，睡蕉小猴的爱巢！
t_en: An island full of bananas, a lovely nest for Slumbernanas!

t_ch: 蕉蕉蕉蕉~蕉蕉蕉蕉~
t_en: ♪ Ba na na na~ Ba na na na~ ♪

t_ch: ——吃完香蕉就睡觉，快快乐乐没烦恼！
t_en: Eat bananas, then nap away. Worry no more, be glad all day!

t_ch: 蕉蕉蕉蕉~蕉蕉蕉蕉~
t_en: ♪ Ba na na na~ Ba na na na~ ♪

t_ch: 蕉蕉蕉~睡蕉小猴真是好~蕉蕉蕉~幸福美满乐陶陶~
t_en: ♪ Ba na na na, Slumbernana Monkey's best~ Ba na na na, enjoying life without a stress ♪

t_ch: 蕉蕉蕉蕉~蕉蕉蕉蕉……
t_en: ♪ Ba na na na~ Ba na na na... ♪

t_ch: （我刚刚…是不是看见了怪东西？）
t_en: (Did I... just see something strange?)

t_ch: （这里就是折纸大学？不像学校啊，倒是很眼熟。）
t_en: (Is this Paperfold University? It doesn't look much like a school, but it does look somewhat familiar.)

t_ch: （还有，那又是什么？）
t_en: (Also, what's that?)

t_ch: 蕉——蕉！
t_en: Bana, bana!

t_ch: 匹诺康尼还有猴子？
t_en: There are monkeys in Penacony!?

t_ch: 这真的是折纸大学吗？
t_en: Is this really Paperfold University?

t_ch: 入梦池给我干哪来了？
t_en: Where did the Dreampool send me?

t_ch: 蕉——蕉蕉蕉！
t_en: Baaaananana!

t_ch: 面前的猴子不断发出*蕉*声，让你想起刚才的影片。哦——那真是部有趣的影片，还记得主题曲是怎么唱的吗？
t_en: The monkey in front of you keeps making Banagibberish sounds, reminding you of the film you just watched. Oh, what an entertaining one that was! Do you remember how the theme song goes?

t_ch: 蕉蕉蕉蕉~蕉蕉蕉蕉~
t_en: ♪ Ba na na na~ Ba na na na~ ♪

t_ch: 对，就是这个旋律！一起唱吧，你将要打开新世界的大门——
t_en: Yep, that's the tune! Let's sing together and open the door to a new world—

t_ch: 蕉蕉蕉蕉~蕉蕉蕉蕉~
t_en: ♪ Ba na na na~ Ba na na na~ ♪

t_ch: 蕉蕉蕉蕉~蕉蕉蕉蕉~
t_en: ♪ Ba na na na~ Ba na na na~ ♪

t_ch: 太棒了，你真是一个天生的*蕉声*歌手！
t_en: Fantastic! You're a born Banagibberish singer!

t_ch: 我不唱！
t_en: I'm not singing!

t_ch: 你是个坚定的无名客，当然要拒绝这种情况的邀请。可当你张开口，说出的却是——
t_en: You are a resolute Nameless, of course you would refuse such an invitation. But when you open your mouth, what comes out is—

t_ch: 蕉蕉蕉！
t_en: Bana bana!

t_ch: 你是谁？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 我？我是你漫长旅途中疲惫不堪的灵魂，是你想要一场休憩的渴望。
t_en: Me? I'm the weary soul on your arduous journey, the longing for a moment of rest.

t_ch: 看！你的*蕉声*吸引来了更多可爱的猴子。
t_en: Look! Your Banagibberish has attracted more adorable monkeys.

t_ch: 蕉蕉，蕉蕉！
t_en: Bana bana bana!

t_ch: 蕉蕉蕉蕉~蕉蕉蕉蕉~
t_en: ♪ Ba na na na~ Ba na na na~ ♪

t_ch: 多么奇妙但又融洽的氛围！无论如何，你应该加入其中，拥抱小猴子们的悦耳*蕉声*和香蕉气息——
t_en: What a wonderful yet harmonious atmosphere! No matter what, you should join in, embrace the pleasant Banagibberish and the scent of bananas from the little monkeys—
